scenario_brief:
Test Scenario: Mixed Room Sizes

This scenario demonstrates the grid system's ability to handle rooms of different sizes and properly align doors between them.

Room Sizes Used:
- 1×1 GU (Closet): 5×6 tiles = 160×192px (small_room_1x1gu)
- 2×1 GU (Hall): 10×6 tiles = 320×192px (hall_1x2gu)
- 2×2 GU (Standard): 10×10 tiles = 320×320px (room_reception, room_ceo)

Layout:
    [Closet-1×1] [CEO-2×2]
           ↑        ↑
      [Wide Hall-2×1]
             ↑
      [Reception-2×2]

Tests:
- Different room sizes in same scenario
- Door alignment between different-sized rooms
- Centering of smaller rooms on larger rooms
- Wide horizontal hallway (2×1 GU)
- Grid-based positioning with varied dimensions

objects.name: Size Guide
objects.text:
Mixed Room Sizes Test

Room Size Reference:
- 1x1 GU (Closet): 5×6 tiles = 160×192px
- 1x2 GU (Hall): 5×10 tiles = 160×320px  
- 2x2 GU (Standard): 10×10 tiles = 320×320px
- 4x1 GU (Wide Hall): 20×6 tiles = 640×192px

Valid heights follow: 2 + (N × 4)
Valid: 6, 10, 14, 18, 22, 26...
Invalid: 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13...

All widths must be multiples of 5 tiles.

objects.name: Hallway Note
objects.text:
Wide Hallway - 2×1 GU (10×6 tiles)

This is a horizontal hallway room demonstrating:
- 2 grid units wide × 1 grid unit tall
- 320×192 pixels (10×6 tiles at 32px/tile)
- Connects to two different sized rooms to the north

The hall connects to:
- Closet (1×1 GU) to the northwest
- CEO Office (2×2 GU) to the northeast

Doors should align properly despite size differences.

objects.name: Closet Info
objects.text:
Storage Closet - 1×1 GU (5×6 tiles)

This is the smallest valid room size:
- 5 tiles wide (1 GU)
- 6 tiles tall (2 + 1×4)
- 160×192 pixels

Perfect for closets, small storage, or utility rooms.

This demonstrates door alignment between a 1×1 GU room and the 2×1 GU hallway below.

objects.name: Success!
objects.text:
CEO Office - Mixed Sizes Test Complete!

This scenario successfully demonstrates:
- Multiple room sizes in one layout:
  • 1×1 GU closet (5×6 tiles)
  • 2×1 GU wide hall (10×6 tiles)
  • 2×2 GU standard rooms (10×10 tiles)
- Door alignment between different-sized rooms
- Grid-based positioning system
- Proper centering of smaller rooms on larger rooms
- Horizontal hallway connector

Room Size Formula:
Width: Must be multiple of 5 tiles
Height: Must be 2 + (N × 4) where N ≥ 1

Validated Examples:
- 5×6 (1×1 GU) ✓ Closet
- 10×6 (2×1 GU) ✓ Wide Hall
- 10×10 (2×2 GU) ✓ Standard
- 10×8 ✗ (8 is invalid: not 2+4N)
- 7×6 ✗ (7 not multiple of 5)
